content.problemStatement:
The Exunites are preparing for the 202520252025 edition of their annual event, Exun!

After a long day of work, they go to the canteen to buy refreshments.

At the canteen, Chef is selling NNN pizzas. However, the Exunites only require KKK pizzas.

Each pizza costs RRR rupees.

Chef wants to know: after selling KKK pizzas to the Exunites, how much revenue can he obtain by selling all his remaining pizzas?


Input Format
The first and only line of input contains 333 space-separated integers N,K,N, K,N,K, and RRR — the number of pizzas Chef is selling, the number of pizzas required by the Exunites, and the cost of each pizza.



Output Format
Print a single integer — the amount of money (in Rupees) Chef's remaining pizzas can sell for.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Chef has 101010 pizzas, the Exunites buy 666 of the pizzas so he has 10−6=410 - 6 = 410−6=4 pizzas left.
Each of these pizzas sell for 151515 rupees, so the total revenue from selling all of the remaining pizzas is 4⋅15=604 \cdot 15 = 604⋅15=60 rupees.

Explanation:
Chef has 252525 pizzas, the Exunites buy 181818 of the pizzas so he has 25−18=725 - 18 = 725−18=7 pizzas left.
Each of these pizzas sell for 202020 rupees, so the total revenue from selling all of the remaining pizzas is 7⋅20=1407 \cdot 20 = 1407⋅20=140 rupees.
